name: Tour of Lac de Thésauque
region_intro: Hiking around Montgeard offers diverse landscapes characterized by rolling hills and picturesque rural scenery within the Lauragais region. The area features the prominent Lac de la Thésauque, a 33-hectare artificial lake, providing a verdant setting for trails. Agricultural landscapes, including fields of "Pastel de Montgeard," add a unique visual element to the natural surroundings. On clear days, distant views of the Pyrenees mountains can be seen from higher points. Best hiking trails around Montgeard The most popular hiking route is Tour of Lac de Thésauque, a 3.3 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a circuit around the lake with waterside views. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Lac de la Thésauque – Lake Thésauque loop from Nailloux, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail explores the lake's surroundings, starting from Nailloux and featuring varied terrain. Local hikers also love the View of Montgeard – Thésauque Lake, Mirepoix loop from Seyre, an 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through the Lauragais countryside and near Lac de la Thésauque, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Montgeard is defined by serene lake paths, rolling hills, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Montgeard are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 70 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Montgeard's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Montgeard?
A: Montgeard offers a wide selection of hiking trails, with over 50 routes to explore. These range from easy strolls to more challenging excursions, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Montgeard?
A: The hiking trails around Montgeard feature diverse landscapes, primarily characterized by rolling hills and picturesque rural scenery within the Lauragais region. Many routes also include serene waterside paths, particularly around the Lac de la Thésauque, and agricultural fields, including those dedicated to 'Pastel de Montgeard'.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Montgeard?
A: Yes, Montgeard has several easy routes suitable for families. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958383713/tour-of-lac-de-thesauque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Lac de Thésauque&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.4 km (3.3 miles) trail that circles the lake, offering pleasant waterside views and a relatively flat path.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Montgeard?
A: Many of the trails around Montgeard are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20535753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lac de la Thésauque – Lake Thésauque loop from Montgeard&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.2 km (6.3 miles) loop that explores the lake's surroundings directly from the village. The region research also mentions 'Le pastel et les herbes folles', a 10 km loop starting and ending in Montgeard.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Montgeard?
A: The best times for hiking in Montgeard are generally spring and autumn, when the weather is mild and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Spring brings blooming fields, while autumn offers beautiful colors. On clear days, especially outside of peak summer heat, you can enjoy distant views of the Pyrenees mountains from higher points.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking near Montgeard?
A: While hiking near Montgeard, you can explore several interesting points. The prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1437834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Ganguise&lt;/a&gt; is nearby, and you might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3759523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hers Crossing&lt;/a&gt;. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesquieu-Lauragais&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4021627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgeard and its church&lt;/a&gt; are also notable sights. The region's agricultural landscapes, including fields of 'Pastel de Montgeard', add a unique cultural element to your walk.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Montgeard dog-friendly?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails around Montgeard, especially in the rural Lauragais countryside and around the Lac de la Thésauque. However, it is always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly near livestock, in nature reserves, or in areas with other hikers, to ensure the safety and enjoyment of everyone.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Montgeard?
A: The hiking routes in Montgeard are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the serene lake paths, the picturesque rolling hills, and the diverse agricultural landscapes. The variety of options, from easy strolls to moderate excursions, is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer experience, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29068033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montgeard – Thésauque Lake, Mirepoix loop from Seyre&lt;/a&gt; offer an 13.1 km (8.1 miles) journey through the Lauragais countryside and near Lac de la Thésauque, typically taking around 3 hours 30 minutes to complete.

Q: Where can I find amenities like food or accommodation near the hiking trails?
A: Montgeard village itself offers some local amenities. Additionally, nearby towns like Nailloux, which is connected by trails such as 'Les deux bastides royales', provide more options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. The Lac de la Thésauque also has leisure facilities that may include seasonal food options.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trails in Montgeard?
A: While Montgeard is primarily accessed by car, some regional bus services might connect to nearby larger towns. For specific trailheads, public transport options are limited, so planning for personal vehicle use or local taxi services is generally recommended for convenient access to the hiking routes.
tips: Far from the hustle and bustle of the parking lot of the Lac de Thésauque leisure area, the departure from the church of Montgeard is a more bucolic way of approaching the tour of the lake with the passage over a high point which allows a glimpse of keep an eye on the Pyrenees, we never get tired of them...
